--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,118 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ce80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Replace structural cascade with geometric layer-wide cardinal nav
+---
+## What
+
+Replace the two-iteration structural cascade in `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs::cardinal_cascade` with a single layer-wide geometric search. Cardinal nav from `focused` in direction D returns the registered scope (leaf or zone, in the same layer) whose rect is geometrically nearest to `focused` in direction D, scored by Android beam (`13 * major² + minor²`). Structural facts (`parent_zone`, kind) are used only to resolve ties and to enforce the layer boundary.
+
+This supersedes per-bug regression tasks `01KQPW1FTYFWTDMW6ESM5ABGJQ` (Left from leftmost perspective tab → no LeftNav landing) and `01KQQSC4D3RSA2PQT9KN1MSXZ3` (Up from column → no perspective bar landing). Both are symptoms of the same root cause: the structural cascade only escalates ONE zone-tree level, and the visible neighbor often lives 2–3 levels above. Mark those tasks as covered by this one and link them via `depends_on` in reverse (those tasks depend on this).
+
+### Current state — why the cascade keeps producing `target=None`
+
+`navigate.rs::cardinal_cascade` (lines 320–391) does exactly two things:
+
+1. **Iter 0** (`beam_among_in_zone_any_kind`, line 614): candidates share the focused entry's `parent_zone`. Any kind.
+2. **Iter 1** (`beam_among_siblings` with `expect_zone=true`, line 576): escalate to the focused entry's parent zone, search ITS sibling zones (only zones; structural).
+
+That's it. Drill-out fallback returns the parent zone's FQM if both miss. Then `drill_into_natural_leaf` (line 414) descends one level into the destination zone to land on a paintable leaf.
+
+For `column:todo` → Up:
+- Iter 0: no in-zone peer above the column header inside `ui:board`. Miss.
+- Iter 1: escalates to `ui:board`. Search peer zones of `ui:board` (sibling zones sharing `ui:board`'s parent). `ui:board`'s parent is `ui:view` (or `ui:perspective` after `01KQPW61ZNS010FV8PV844KY0K` lands), and `ui:view` has no other zone children. Miss.
+- Drill-out fallback: return `ui:view`'s FQM. The React glue then maps that FQM through `ui.setFocus` and somewhere along the way it becomes `target=None` — the symptom captured in the bug logs.
+
+`ui:perspective-bar` lives at the layer root, three zone-tree levels above `column:todo`. The cascade only ever escalates ONCE. There is no path through the structural cascade that reaches it. Same shape for the `Left from leftmost tab → ui:left-nav` bug. **No amount of edge-case patching fixes this — the algorithm can't express "look across the whole layer."**
+
+### General principle
+
+> Cardinal nav from `focused` in direction D returns the registered scope (leaf or zone) within the same `layer_fq` whose rect minimises the Android beam score (`13 * major² + minor²`) **across ALL registered scopes in the layer that pass the in-beam test for direction D and lie strictly in the half-plane of D**. Structural fields (`parent_zone`, `is_zone`) are not candidate filters; they are tie-breakers and observability only.
+
+Properties this preserves:
+
+- **In-zone peers still win** because they have lower beam scores by virtue of being closer.
+- **`navOverride` walls/redirects still run first** (rule 0) — unchanged.
+- **No-silent-dropout contract** still holds: when the half-plane is genuinely empty (focused entry sits at the visual edge of the layer), return `focused_fq` (stay-put). Never return `None`, never collapse to engine root.
+- **Layer boundary** is still the hard wall — `candidate.layer_fq == focused.layer_fq` is the first filter applied to the global candidate set.
+
+Properties this changes:
+
+- **Cross-zone navigation just works.** `column:todo → Up → perspective_tab:p1` (or `filter_editor:...`) succeeds because `perspective_tab:*` and `filter_editor:*` are in the same layer and lie in the upward half-plane of `column:todo`'s rect.
+- **The "iter 0 / iter 1 / drill-out" cascade goes away.** Cardinal nav becomes one function: `geometric_pick(layer, from_rect, direction)`.
+- **`drill_into_natural_leaf`** moves from "post-cascade fixup" to "applied whenever the geometric pick lands on a `showFocusBar=false` zone" — same purpose, simpler trigger condition. (Or: drop it entirely if the geometric pick already prefers leaves over zones via tie-break.)
+
+### What zones still earn after this change
+
+Zones today carry FIVE responsibilities (per the cascade walk above and `scope.rs`):
+
+1. **Bound cardinal nav** — `parent_zone` filters iter 0/1 candidates.
+2. **Drill-in target** — Enter descends into a zone, lands on its first leaf.
+3. **Drill-out target** — Escape ascends to the parent zone.
+4. **Last-focused memory** — restored when you re-enter a zone.
+5. **Layer membership** — actually owned by `FocusLayer`, not `FocusZone`. Crossed off; this is a different primitive.
+
+Geometric cardinal nav drops responsibility (1) entirely. (2), (3), (4) all stay because they're about *containment*, not *direction*. So zones still earn their keep — but for fewer reasons. Today zones do double duty (structure AND navigation); after this change they do only structure, which is a clarifying simplification.
+
+A more aggressive follow-up — collapsing `FocusZone` and `FocusScope` into one primitive with optional children, so drill-in works on any primitive with children — is **out of scope here**. File separately if interesting.
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`:
+  - Replace `cardinal_cascade` body (line 320) with `geometric_pick(reg, focused, focused_fq, direction)`.
+  - The new function iterates `reg.entries_in_layer(focused.layer_fq())`, filters out the focused entry itself, scores each via the existing `score_candidate` (line 713) which already enforces the half-plane and in-beam tests, and returns the lowest-scoring candidate's FQM. On empty result set, return `focused_fq` (stay-put per the no-silent-dropout contract).
+  - Tie-breaks (when two candidates produce equal scores): prefer `is_zone == false` (leaf wins over zone), then prefer the candidate whose ancestry shares more with `focused` (so in-zone neighbors win ties against cross-zone ones). The leaf-preference tie-break replaces the role of `drill_into_natural_leaf` for the common case.
+  - Keep `drill_into_natural_leaf` as a post-pick step ONLY when the winner is a `showFocusBar=false` zone with leaf descendants — otherwise the leaf-tie-break already produces the right thing.
+  - Delete `beam_among_in_zone_any_kind`, `beam_among_siblings`, `parent_zone_resolution`, `ParentResolution` — all unused after the cascade is replaced.
+  - Keep `score_candidate`, `pick_best_candidate`, the in-beam helpers, and the override/edge-command paths unchanged.
+  - Update the module-level docstring to describe the geometric algorithm and explicitly note "structural is for tie-breaks, not filtering."
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/README.md` — rewrite the "## The cascade" section. Replace the three-step cascade prose with the geometric-pick prose. Keep the "## Edge commands", "## Overrides", "## No-silent-dropout", and "## Kind is not a filter" sections — those still hold (the new algorithm is even more committed to "kind is not a filter").
+
+### Tests to revisit (this is the load-bearing risk)
+
+The current cascade exists in part to preserve specific user-visible wins. The geometric algorithm must keep those wins or knowingly trade them. Walk every existing test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/` and confirm:
+
+- `card_directional_nav.rs` — within-card moves between drag-leaf, title-zone, inspect-leaf, tags-zone. The geometric algorithm SHOULD produce the same answers because the geometrically-nearest neighbor inside a card IS the structurally-nearest one (cards are small, well-laid-out boxes). Verify.
+- `column_header_arrow_nav.rs` — `up_from_t1a_lands_on_column_name_zone`, etc. Geometric should agree because the column name field is the geometrically-nearest scope above the topmost card.
+- `navbar_arrow_nav.rs` and `perspective_bar_arrow_nav.rs` — in-row leaf navigation. Geometric agrees for the same reason.
+- `unified_trajectories.rs` — multi-step paths through the realistic-app fixture. Run as-is; if any step changes, evaluate whether the new behaviour is BETTER (visually nearer neighbor) or worse (skips a structurally-meaningful waypoint).
+- `in_zone_any_kind_first.rs` — pins the iter-0 anti-pattern callout (no kind filter). Geometric is stricter on this principle, so these tests should still pass.
+- `overrides.rs` — rule 0 unchanged.
+- `no_silent_none.rs` — the no-silent-dropout contract. Geometric must still return `focused_fq` for genuine "no motion possible" cases (focused at the visual edge of the layer). Add cases.
+- `drill.rs` and `inspector_dismiss.rs` — drill-in/out and modal layer behaviour. Unaffected by cardinal nav changes.
+
+### Files to read first (for the implementer)
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` (full file).
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/README.md` ("## The cascade", "## Kind is not a filter").
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/fixtures/mod.rs` — the realistic-app fixture used by most integration tests. Note it currently lacks `ui:left-nav` and column zones inside `ui:board`; both should be added (this work was already noted in `01KQPW1FTYFWTDMW6ESM5ABGJQ`).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `BeamNavStrategy::next` for cardinal directions runs a single layer-wide geometric search; the iter-0 / iter-1 / drill-out cascade is gone from `cardinal_cascade`.
+- [ ] All four reported cross-zone bugs resolve via the same code:
+  - [ ] Left from leftmost `perspective_tab:*` lands on a leaf inside `ui:left-nav` (or `ui:left-nav` itself if it has no leaves).
+  - [ ] Up from `column:{id}` lands on a leaf inside `ui:perspective-bar` (or one of its leaves: `perspective_tab:*` or `filter_editor:*`).
+  - [ ] Down from a `perspective_tab:*` lands inside the perspective body (board column, grid cell, etc.) when one is below — not on the same tab or no-op.
+  - [ ] Up from any board column header lands on the perspective bar; Up from the perspective bar lands on the navbar.
+- [ ] Every existing test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/` either passes unchanged OR is updated to reflect a deliberate semantic change (with rationale in the commit message). No test is silently deleted.
+- [ ] No-silent-dropout contract still holds: `next` always returns a `FullyQualifiedMoniker`, never an `Option`, never the FQM of a different layer, never `target=None` in the IPC.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` passes.
+- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` passes (the React-side cross-zone integration tests in `spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx` and similar should now exercise the new behaviour).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] **New unit tests** in `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs::tests` exercising `geometric_pick` directly: lonely scope returns focused FQM; one neighbor in direction wins; two neighbors at different distances — closer wins; tied distances — leaf wins over zone; cross-`parent_zone` candidate wins when geometrically nearer than in-zone candidate.
+- [ ] **New integration test** `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/cross_zone_geometric_nav.rs`: extend the realistic-app fixture (or use it as-is once `ui:left-nav` and `column:*` zones are added per `01KQPW1FTYFWTDMW6ESM5ABGJQ`) to assert the four reported bugs all return the visibly-adjacent target. Each direction × starting-FQM combination is one test.
+- [ ] **Regression sweep**: re-run every test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/`. For any failure, write a short note in the PR description explaining whether the new behaviour is correct (and the test should be updated) or wrong (and `geometric_pick` needs work).
+- [ ] **End-to-end** `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx`: add a test that drives ArrowUp from a column header in the production-shaped harness and asserts focus lands inside `[data-segment^="perspective_tab:"]` or `[data-segment^="filter_editor:"]`. Asserts the `ui.setFocus` IPC carries a non-`None` target.
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus && pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test spatial-nav-end-to-end` and confirm green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- This is the design + implementation of a kernel algorithm change. Use `/tdd` strictly: write the four cross-zone regression tests first against the current cascade (RED), implement `geometric_pick`, watch the four go green, then sweep the existing test suite and resolve each delta with intent.
+- The follow-up "should we collapse `FocusZone` and `FocusScope` into one primitive" question is **not** part of this task — file separately if the geometric change motivates it.

`ui:perspective-bar` lives at the layer root, three zone-tree levels above `column:todo`. The cascade only ever escalates ONCE. There is no path through the structural cascade that reaches it. Same shape for the `Left from leftmost tab → ui:left-nav` bug. **No amount of edge-case patching fixes this — the algorithm can't express "look across the whole layer."**

### General principle

> Cardinal nav from `focused` in direction D returns the registered scope (leaf or zone) within the same `layer_fq` whose rect minimises the Android beam score (`13 * major² + minor²`) **across ALL registered scopes in the layer that pass the in-beam test for direction D and lie strictly in the half-plane of D**. Structural fields (`parent_zone`, `is_zone`) are not candidate filters; they are tie-breakers and observability only.

Properties this preserves:

- **In-zone peers still win** because they have lower beam scores by virtue of being closer.
- **`navOverride` walls/redirects still run first** (rule 0) — unchanged.
- **No-silent-dropout contract** still holds: when the half-plane is genuinely empty (focused entry sits at the visual edge of the layer), return `focused_fq` (stay-put). Never return `None`, never collapse to engine root.
- **Layer boundary** is still the hard wall — `candidate.layer_fq == focused.layer_fq` is the first filter applied to the global candidate set.

Properties this changes:

- **Cross-zone navigation just works.** `column:todo → Up → perspective_tab:p1` (or `filter_editor:...`) succeeds because `perspective_tab:*` and `filter_editor:*` are in the same layer and lie in the upward half-plane of `column:todo`'s rect.
- **The "iter 0 / iter 1 / drill-out" cascade goes away.** Cardinal nav becomes one function: `geometric_pick(layer, from_rect, direction)`.
- **`drill_into_natural_leaf`** moves from "post-cascade fixup" to "applied whenever the geometric pick lands on a `showFocusBar=false` zone" — same purpose, simpler trigger condition. (Or: drop it entirely if the geometric pick already prefers leaves over zones via tie-break.)

### What zones still earn after this change

Zones today carry FIVE responsibilities (per the cascade walk above and `scope.rs`):

1. **Bound cardinal nav** — `parent_zone` filters iter 0/1 candidates.
2. **Drill-in target** — Enter descends into a zone, lands on its first leaf.
3. **Drill-out target** — Escape ascends to the parent zone.
4. **Last-focused memory** — restored when you re-enter a zone.
5. **Layer membership** — actually owned by `FocusLayer`, not `FocusZone`. Crossed off; this is a different primitive.

Geometric cardinal nav drops responsibility (1) entirely. (2), (3), (4) all stay because they're about *containment*, not *direction*. So zones still earn their keep — but for fewer reasons. Today zones do double duty (structure AND navigation); after this change they do only structure, which is a clarifying simplification.

A more aggressive follow-up — collapsing `FocusZone` and `FocusScope` into one primitive with optional children, so drill-in works on any primitive with children — is **out of scope here**. File separately if interesting.

### Files to modify

- `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`:

  - Tie-breaks (when two candidates produce equal scores): prefer `is_zone == false` (leaf wins over zone), then prefer the candidate whose ancestry shares more with `focused` (so in-zone neighbors win ties against cross-zone ones). The leaf-preference tie-break replaces the role of `drill_into_natural_leaf` for the common case.
  - Keep `drill_into_natural_leaf` as a post-pick step ONLY when the winner is a `showFocusBar=false` zone with leaf descendants — otherwise the leaf-tie-break already produces the right thing.
  - Delete `beam_among_in_zone_any_kind`, `beam_among_siblings`, `parent_zone_resolution`, `ParentResolution` — all unused after the cascade is replaced.
  - Keep `score_candidate`, `pick_best_candidate`, the in-beam helpers, and the override/edge-command paths unchanged.
  - Update the module-level docstring to describe the geometric algorithm and explicitly note "structural is for tie-breaks, not filtering."

Keep the "## Edge commands", "## Overrides", "## No-silent-dropout", and "## Kind is not a filter" sections — those still hold (the new algorithm is even more committed to "kind is not a filter").

+### Nested scrollable regions — three behaviours to preserve
+
+The app has nested scroll containers: each board column scrolls vertically (cards), the column strip scrolls horizontally (columns), the grid scrolls both ways, and the inspector is its own scroll container on a separate layer. The geometric model handles them uniformly *if* registered rects are current (post-scroll).
+
+**1. Off-screen but registered — works for free.** A card scrolled below the viewport still has a registered rect in layout coordinates. Geometric nav picks it as the nearest below; `scrollIntoView` (the existing `useTrackRectOnAncestorScroll` listener already keeps rects current and the focus-claim handler already calls `scrollIntoView`) brings it into view. **No kernel change needed** — confirm by writing a regression test that drives Down from the last visible card and asserts the next-below card both gains focus and gets scrolled into view.

+**2. Cross-scroll-container nav — works for free.** Right from the rightmost card in `column:todo` should land on the leftmost card of `column:doing`, with the column strip scrolling horizontally if needed. Geometric pick finds the next column's card naturally; `scrollIntoView` walks scroll parents and handles the horizontal scroll. **No kernel change needed.** Add a regression test.
+
+**3. Virtualized regions — needs a decision.** If a list uses windowed rendering and only visible rows mount their `<FocusScope>`, rows outside the window have no registered scope. Geometric nav from the last visible row → Down finds nothing → stay-put. User is stuck.
+
+Two viable approaches; pick one in this task or document deferral:
+
+- **Option A: register everything.** Virtualized lists register a `<FocusScope>` for every item with a layout-coordinate rect, even off-viewport ones. Heavy memory for huge lists but the kernel works uniformly. Recommended unless current virtualization infra (`data-table.tsx`'s use of `@tanstack/react-virtual`?) makes this prohibitively expensive.
+- **Option B: scroll-on-edge fallback.** Add a sixth primitive: when geometric nav returns stay-put AND the focused scope is at the edge of a scrollable ancestor in direction D, scroll the ancestor by one item-height in direction D (no focus change). The next keypress re-runs nav, finds the freshly-mounted scope, and lands. Cleaner for huge lists; slightly less obvious as a model.

+title: 'DESIGN: Spatial nav — keyboard-as-mouse cardinal + drill / first / last on Scope primitive'
 ---
 ## What

-Redesign spatial focus navigation around five generic operations on a single `Scope` primitive. Goal: replace the current cascade (which has accumulated special cases — iter 0 / iter 1 / drill-out / cross-zone drill-in / zone-vs-leaf split) with a small, elegant set of geometric / containment primitives that together explain every existing user-visible behaviour AND fix the cross-zone bug class that currently produces `target=None` collapses.
+This is the **design spec** for a redesigned spatial focus navigation system. It defines the contract; six component tasks (listed below) implement the contract piecewise.

 User's mental model: **"directional nav is keyboard-as-mouse"** — pressing an arrow key picks the visually-nearest thing in that direction, regardless of structural depth. Containers (zones) earn their keep only for the **drill** / **first** / **last** operations, where the tree shape matters.

-This supersedes per-bug regression tasks `01KQPW1FTYFWTDMW6ESM5ABGJQ` and `01KQQSC4D3RSA2PQT9KN1MSXZ3` — both are symptoms of the same structural-cascade limitation.
+This redesign supersedes the structural-cascade bug class — symptoms include `target=None`, `scope_chain=["engine"]`, focus collapsing to root when pressing Left from the leftmost perspective tab or Up from a board column. Two per-bug regression tasks (`01KQPW1FTYFWTDMW6ESM5ABGJQ` and `01KQQSC4D3RSA2PQT9KN1MSXZ3`) are archived; their scenarios are now acceptance criteria below.
+
+## Contract

 ### The five primitive operations

 | Operation | Key | Behaviour |
-|-----------|-----|-----------|
-| `nav.{up,down,left,right}` | Arrow keys | **Geometric**: pick the registered scope (in the same layer) whose rect is nearest to the focused scope in direction D, scored by Android beam (`13 * major² + minor²`). **No structural filtering.** Just the visually-closest neighbor in the half-plane of D. |
-| `nav.drillIn` | Enter | If focused scope has children, focus its first child (geometric: topmost-then-leftmost). If no children, no-op (return focused FQM). |
-| `nav.drillOut` | Escape | Focus the parent scope. If focused at layer root, fall through to `app.dismiss` (close topmost modal layer). |
-| `nav.first` | Home | If focused scope has children, focus its first child (same as drillIn). Otherwise no-op. |
-| `nav.last` | End | If focused scope has children, focus its last child (geometric: bottommost-then-rightmost). Otherwise no-op. |
+|---|---|---|
+| `nav.{up,down,left,right}` | Arrows | **Geometric**: pick the registered scope (in the same layer) whose rect is nearest to the focused scope in direction D, scored by Android beam (`13 * major² + minor²`). **No structural filtering.** Just the visually-closest neighbor in the half-plane of D. |
+| `nav.drillIn` | Enter | Focus the focused scope's first child (geometric: topmost-then-leftmost). On a leaf, no-op (return focused FQM). |
+| `nav.drillOut` | Escape | Focus the parent scope. At layer root, fall through to `app.dismiss`. |
+| `nav.first` | Home | Same as drillIn (focus first child). On a leaf, no-op. |
+| `nav.last` | End | Focus the last child (bottommost-then-rightmost). On a leaf, no-op. |
+
+### Invariants

-That's the entire model. Layer boundaries are still the hard wall (no operation crosses `layer_fq`). `navOverride` walls/redirects still run before any of these (rule 0).
+- **Layer is the hard wall.** No operation crosses `layer_fq`. The inspector layer remains isolated from the main layer.
+- **`navOverride` runs first** (rule 0). Per-direction redirects and walls short-circuit the cascade entirely.
+- **No-silent-dropout.** Every operation returns a `FullyQualifiedMoniker` — never `None`, never `target=None` in the IPC, never collapses to engine root. "No motion possible" is signalled by returning the focused FQM (stay-put).
+- **Coordinate system: viewport-relative.** All registered rects are sampled by `getBoundingClientRect()` and refreshed on ancestor scroll via `useTrackRectOnAncestorScroll`. The kernel's geometric pick is correct iff all candidate rects in the same layer were sampled in the same coordinate system. **This invariant is load-bearing — see component task #6 (coordinate consistency).**

 ### Why this is elegant

-**Cardinal nav is keyboard-as-mouse.** The user points at things visually with arrow keys. No mental model of zone hierarchies needed. The visually-nearest scope wins. This solves the entire class of bugs where the cascade can't reach a scope that's geometrically obvious because the structural path isn't right (Left from leftmost tab → LeftNav, Up from column → perspective bar, etc.).
+- **Cardinal nav is keyboard-as-mouse.** No mental model of zone hierarchies needed; the visually-nearest scope wins. Solves the cross-zone bug class because geometric distance doesn't care about structural depth.
+- **Drill / first / last anchor on scope children.** That's why scopes (containers) still earn their keep — they define what "first child" and "last child" mean.
+- **Five ops, one rule per op.** No iter 0 vs iter 1, no any-kind vs same-kind filter, no drill-into-natural-leaf as a post-cascade fixup.

-**Drill / first / last are the only operations where containment matters.** That's why scopes (containers) still earn their keep — they define what "first child" and "last child" mean. Without containers, those operations would have no anchor.
+### Virtualization

-**Five ops, no special cases.** No iter 0 vs iter 1. No same-kind vs any-kind filter. No drill-into-natural-leaf as a post-cascade fixup. The geometric pick is one function. Drill / first / last all key on the focused scope's children.
+**Essential** — the app uses windowed rendering and off-viewport rows do not register `<FocusScope>`. The kernel cannot find them via geometric pick. The fix is a **scroll-on-edge** rule that lives in the React glue, not the kernel:

-### Nested scrollable regions — three behaviours to preserve
+> When the kernel returns stay-put AND the focused scope is at the edge of a scrollable ancestor in direction D AND that ancestor can scroll further in D, scroll it by one item-height in D, wait for the virtualizer to mount the next row, then re-run nav.

-The app has nested scroll containers: each board column scrolls vertically (cards), the column strip scrolls horizontally (columns), the grid scrolls both ways, and the inspector is its own scroll container on a separate layer. The geometric model handles them uniformly *if* registered rects are current (post-scroll).
+This is component task #5.

-**1. Off-screen but registered — works for free.** A card scrolled below the viewport still has a registered rect in layout coordinates. Geometric nav picks it as the nearest below; `scrollIntoView` (the existing `useTrackRectOnAncestorScroll` listener already keeps rects current and the focus-claim handler already calls `scrollIntoView`) brings it into view. **No kernel change needed** — confirm by writing a regression test that drives Down from the last visible card and asserts the next-below card both gains focus and gets scrolled into view.
+### Containment tree contract (for drill / first / last)

-**2. Cross-scroll-container nav — works for free.** Right from the rightmost card in `column:todo` should land on the leftmost card of `column:doing`, with the column strip scrolling horizontally if needed. Geometric pick finds the next column's card naturally; `scrollIntoView` walks scroll parents and handles the horizontal scroll. **No kernel change needed.** Add a regression test.
+- **First child** = the child whose rect is topmost; ties broken by leftmost.
+- **Last child** = the child whose rect is bottommost; ties broken by rightmost.
+- **Parent** = the focused scope's `parent_zone` (after `FocusZone` / `FocusScope` distinction is preserved at the type level — see component task #2 / #4 for whether the kernel exposes `parent_scope` more generally).
+- **Children** = registered scopes whose `parent_zone` is the focused scope's FQM.

-**3. Virtualized regions — needs a decision.** If a list uses windowed rendering and only visible rows mount their `<FocusScope>`, rows outside the window have no registered scope. Geometric nav from the last visible row → Down finds nothing → stay-put. User is stuck.
+If the type-level `FocusZone` / `FocusScope` split is collapsed into one `Scope` primitive (separate follow-up, NOT in this plan), drill / first / last work on any scope's children — leaves just have zero children.

-Two viable approaches; pick one in this task or document deferral:
+## Component tasks (links to follow as they're filed)

-- **Option A: register everything.** Virtualized lists register a `<FocusScope>` for every item with a layout-coordinate rect, even off-viewport ones. Heavy memory for huge lists but the kernel works uniformly. Recommended unless current virtualization infra (`data-table.tsx`'s use of `@tanstack/react-virtual`?) makes this prohibitively expensive.
-- **Option B: scroll-on-edge fallback.** Add a sixth primitive: when geometric nav returns stay-put AND the focused scope is at the edge of a scrollable ancestor in direction D, scroll the ancestor by one item-height in direction D (no focus change). The next keypress re-runs nav, finds the freshly-mounted scope, and lands. Cleaner for huge lists; slightly less obvious as a model.

+### Open question to resolve during implementation: collapse `FocusZone` / `FocusScope` into one `Scope` primitive?
+
+Today the kernel has TWO primitive types:
+
+- `FocusZone` — has children, is a navigation container.
+- `FocusScope` — leaf, no children.
+
+The new model only needs ONE: a scope that has zero or more children. Drill / first / last operate on the focused scope's children (no-op if none). Cardinal nav doesn't care about kind at all.
